framework_selection.reasoning: Paper explicitly uses PyTorch (Appendix E.5: 'All models are implemented in PyTorch'). Official catnat implementation at github.com/allemanenti/catnat-torch also uses PyTorch.
framework_selection.config_management: YAML + dataclasses (lightweight; no Hydra dependency needed for this scope)

src/catnat/.__init__.py: Package root; exports CatNat, NaturalActivation, SoftmaxBaseline, SparseMax

public_methods.NaturalActivation.forward(x: Tensor) ->: Applies ν(x) elementwise; saturates outside [C-A/2, C+A/2]
public_methods.NaturalActivation.fim_diagonal_factor(s:: Returns P(a_i)*(π/A)^2 — the diagonal FIM entry for diagnostics

public_methods.CatNat.__init__(K: int, activation: str): K must be power of 2; activation in ['natural','sigmoid']
public_methods.CatNat.forward(s: Tensor) -> Tensor: s: [B, K-1] -> p: [B, K]; builds leaf probs via tree traversal
public_methods.CatNat.log_prob(s: Tensor) -> Tensor: Returns log-probabilities [B, K] for numerical stability
public_methods.CatNat.node_reach_probs(s: Tensor) -> Te: Returns P(a_i) for each internal node [B, K-1] — needed for FIM diagnostics
public_methods.CatNat.fim_diagonal(s: Tensor) -> Tensor: Returns diagonal of FIM [B, K-1] per Theorem 4.2

catnat.py.design_notes: Tree traversal implemented as vectorized tensor ops, NOT a loop over nodes. Node indices stored as a precomputed ancestor mask buffer.

public_methods.GumbelSoftmaxSampler.forward(log_probs:: Gumbel-Softmax with straight-through; hard=True gives one-hot in forward, dense in backward
public_methods.GumbelSoftmaxSampler.anneal_temperature(: Exponential decay: tau_init * exp(-rate * step); clamps to tau_min
public_methods.REINFORCESampler.log_prob(samples: Tenso: Log probability of samples under Cat(probs)

models/.__init__.py: Exports VAEEncoder, VAEDecoder, CatVAE, GCNEncoder, GSLModel, PPOAgent

vae.py.sir_source: Encoder g_θ (CNN), Decoder f_ψ (Transposed CNN) — Section 5.2, Appendix F.1

public_methods.CatVAE.forward(x: Tensor) -> dict: Returns {recon, probs, log_probs, C_hard, C_soft, elbo}

architecture_details.decoder_deconv: Linear(N*K, 512) -> Linear(512,?) -> Unflatten -> ConvTranspose2d x3 -> Sigmoid
architecture_details.note_conv_sizes: ASSUMED from referenced codebase (confidence 0.70) — TODO: verify against github.com/jxmorris12/categorical-vae

gsl.py.sir_source: Encoder g_θ (GCN), Decoder f_ψ (GCN) — Section 5.1, Appendix E

public_methods.GSLModel.sample_graphs(n: int) -> Tensor: Samples n adjacency matrices from Bernoulli latent graph distribution
public_methods.GSLModel.latent_log_prob(A: Tensor) -> T: log P_θ(A) = sum of Bernoulli log-probs

ppo_agent.py.sir_source: Encoder g_θ (CNN), RL — Section 5.3, Appendix I.2

public_methods.PPOActor.forward(features: Tensor) -> Te: features:[B,512] -> action_probs:[B,A] via catnat or softmax
public_methods.PPOAgent.evaluate_actions(obs: Tensor, a: Returns {log_prob, entropy, value} for given actions

public_methods.EnergyScore.forward(preds: Tensor, targe: preds:[M,B,D] target:[B,D] -> scalar ES loss per EQ10

public_methods.GSLTrainer.train(model, dataset, config): Full training loop with LOO baseline REINFORCE
public_methods.PPOTrainer.train(agent, env, config) ->: PPO update loop with GAE advantage estimation

public_methods.FIMAnalyzer.compute_diagonal(model, x) -: Computes diagonal FIM entries via Theorem 4.2
public_methods.FIMAnalyzer.compare_landscape(softmax_mo: Generates loss landscape comparison data (reproduces Figure 1)

fim_analysis.py.note: Optional diagnostic module; not required for reproduction but useful for verifying theoretical claims

public_methods.GSLDataGenerator.generate(n_samples, the: Generates synthetic (x,y) pairs via GCN on latent graph per Appendix E.1

public_methods.AtariPreprocessor.wrap(env) -> gym.Env: Applies grayscale, resize 84x84, frame-stack 4, reward clip [-1,1]

public_methods.BinaryTreeIndex.build_ancestor_mask(K: i: Precomputes [K-1, K-1] boolean mask: mask[i,j]=True iff node j is ancestor of node i. Used for vectorized tree traversal.
public_methods.BinaryTreeIndex.get_leaf_paths(K: int) -: Returns [K, H] binary path matrix — the b_1,...,b_H for each leaf
public_methods.BinaryTreeIndex.validate_K(K: int) -> bo: Checks K is power of 2; raises ValueError otherwise

tree_utils.py.note: Critical utility. The ancestor mask enables O(K log K) vectorized forward pass instead of O(K^2) node-by-node loop.

functions: init_scores_uniform(shape, low=0.0, high=0.1) -> Tensor: GSL score initialization per Appendix E.5
orthogonal_init(module): Orthogonal weight initialization for RL CNN per Appendix I.2
set_seed(seed: int): Sets torch, numpy, random seeds + deterministic CUDA
get_device(config) -> torch.device: Returns cuda/cpu based on config

scripts/.generate_gsl_data.py: Standalone: generates and saves synthetic GSL datasets for all 5 theta* settings
scripts/.run_gsl_grid.py: Runs 2-stage LR grid search for GSL (coarse then refined)
scripts/.run_vae_grid.py: Runs 2-stage LR grid search for VAE across (N,K) configs
scripts/.run_rl_tpe.py: Runs 160-trial TPE hyperparameter search for RL

tests/.test_catnat.py: Unit tests: forward pass correctness, probabilities sum to 1, gradient flow
tests/.test_fim.py: Unit tests: FIM diagonality verification vs numerical estimate
tests/.test_losses.py: Unit tests: Energy Score, KL divergence, ELBO
tests/.test_samplers.py: Unit tests: Gumbel-Softmax output shape, REINFORCE log-prob

tensor_flow_specs.name: CatNat Forward Pass (core — all experiments)
pseudocode: INPUT  s: [B, K-1]  float32  — unnormalized scores from encoder
STEP 1  a = activation(s)                           → [B, K-1]  — Bernoulli probs at each internal node via ν or σ
STEP 2  paths = tree_index.get_leaf_paths(K)        → [K, H]    — precomputed binary path matrix (0/1 for R/L at each level)
STEP 3  a_indexed = gather a along paths            → [B, K, H] — for each leaf, the activation values along its path
STEP 4  flip = where(paths==0, 1-a_indexed, a_indexed) → [B, K, H] — apply (1-a) for right branches, a for left
STEP 5  log_p = sum(log(flip + eps), dim=-1)        → [B, K]    — log-leaf-probabilities (sum of log-factors)
STEP 6  p = exp(log_p)                              → [B, K]    — leaf probabilities; sums to 1 by construction
OUTPUT p: [B, K]  float32
tensor_flow_specs.implementation_note: Steps 3-5 are the vectorized tree traversal using BinaryTreeIndex.ancestor_mask. Log-space computation in Step 5 is mandatory for numerical stability with deep trees (large K).

tensor_flow_specs.name: CatVAE Forward Pass (VAE experiment)
pseudocode: INPUT  x: [B, 1, 28, 28]  float32  — input image
STEP 1  s = encoder(x)                              → [B, N*(K-1)]  — CNN encoder scores
STEP 2  s = reshape(s, [B, N, K-1])                 → [B, N, K-1]
STEP 3  log_p = catnat.log_prob(s)                  → [B, N, K]     — log categorical probabilities
STEP 4  log_p_gumbel = log_p + gumbel_noise         → [B, N, K]     — add Gumbel noise to leaf log-probs
NOTE: Gumbel noise injection is at leaf log-probs (confidence 0.65 — TODO: verify vs catnat-torch codebase)
STEP 5  C_soft = softmax(log_p_gumbel / tau)        → [B, N, K]     — soft relaxation
STEP 6  C_hard = one_hot(argmax(C_soft), K)         → [B, N, K]     — hard one-hot (straight-through)
STEP 7  C = C_hard + (C_soft - C_soft.detach())     → [B, N, K]     — straight-through estimator
STEP 8  x_recon = decoder(C.reshape(B, N*K))        → [B, 1, 28, 28] — reconstructed image via sigmoid
STEP 9  kl = KLDivCategorical(exp(log_p), K)        → scalar          — analytic KL vs Uniform
STEP 10 recon_loss = BCE(x_recon, x)                → scalar
STEP 11 elbo = recon_loss + kl                      → scalar
OUTPUT  {recon: [B,1,28,28], probs: [B,N,K], elbo: scalar}

tensor_flow_specs.name: GSL Training Step (GSL experiment)
pseudocode: INPUT  x: [B, N_nodes, d_in]  — node features
target y: [B, N_nodes, d_out]
STEP 1  theta = catnat.forward(score_params)         → [N_edges, 2]  — Bernoulli probs per edge (binary catnat: K=2)
STEP 2  A_samples = REINFORCE_sampler.sample(theta, M=32)  → [M, B, N_nodes, N_nodes]
STEP 3  y_preds = GNN(x, A_samples)                 → [M, B, N_nodes, d_out]
STEP 4  es_losses = EnergyScore(y_preds, y)          → [M, B]        — per-sample per-batch ES
STEP 5  baseline_b = LOOBaseline(es_losses)          → [M, B]
STEP 6  log_probs = REINFORCE_sampler.log_prob(A_samples, theta) → [M, B]
STEP 7  reinforce_grad_signal = (es_losses - baseline_b) * log_probs  → [M, B]
STEP 8  loss_theta = mean(reinforce_grad_signal)     → scalar
STEP 9  loss_psi = mean(es_losses)                   → scalar         — GNN params use direct gradient
STEP 10 total_loss = loss_psi + loss_theta.detach() * score_params   — combined with stop-gradient trick
OUTPUT  {loss: scalar, es: float, theta_est: Tensor}

tensor_flow_specs.name: PPO Policy Forward Pass (RL experiment)
pseudocode: INPUT  obs: [B, 4, 84, 84]  uint8 → normalize to float32 / 255
STEP 1  features = AtariCNN(obs)                     → [B, 512]
STEP 2  action_scores = actor_head(features)         → [B, K-1]      — K = number of discrete actions, K-1 scores for catnat
STEP 3  action_probs = catnat.forward(action_scores) → [B, K]
STEP 4  action_dist = Categorical(action_probs)
STEP 5  action = action_dist.sample()                → [B]
STEP 6  log_prob = action_dist.log_prob(action)      → [B]
STEP 7  entropy = action_dist.entropy()              → [B]
STEP 8  value = critic_head(features)                → [B, 1]
OUTPUT  {action, log_prob, entropy, value}
tensor_flow_specs.ppo_update_note: PPO clipped surrogate + value loss + entropy bonus per EQ12. Advantages computed via GAE(γ=0.99, λ=0.95) and normalized per mini-batch.

model.latent_dim_comment: N categorical variables each with K classes

dependency_rationale.optuna: TPE hyperparameter search for RL experiment

entrypoints.description: Main training script for all three experiments
cli_args: --N INT  [VAE only; number of categorical variables; default: 20]
--K INT  [VAE only; categories per variable; default: 16]
--compute_fim BOOL  [default: False; runs FIM diagonal analysis]

entrypoints.description: Pre-generates and saves all 5 GSL synthetic datasets
160-trial TPE hyperparameter search for RL; select top-10 configs for final eval

risk_assessment.description: The balanced binary tree structure requires K to be a power of 2. The paper never discusses non-power-of-2 K, but real applications may require arbitrary K.
risk_assessment.mitigation: BinaryTreeIndex.validate_K() raises ValueError for non-powers-of-2. Design CatNat with abstract TreeBuilder base class so alternative tree structures can be plugged in without API changes. Add a 'pad_to_power_of_2' config option that adds dummy categories and masks them in the output.

risk_assessment.description: The paper does not specify where Gumbel noise is added in the catnat+Gumbel-Softmax hybrid. Adding noise to leaf log-probs (our assumption) may differ from adding it at internal node logits, producing different gradient behavior.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement BOTH options as a config flag: gumbel_injection: {leaf_logprobs, node_logits}. Default to leaf_logprobs (our assumption). Add a unit test that checks gradient flow through both paths. Consult catnat-torch reference codebase to confirm before finalizing.

risk_assessment.description: Exact kernel sizes, strides, and channel counts for the VAE encoder/decoder are not specified in the paper. Paper says '3 conv + 2 FC' but no dimensions given.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Fetch and parse the referenced codebase (github.com/jxmorris12/categorical-vae) during Stage 4 to extract exact layer specs. Mark assumed dimensions with # ASSUMED comments. Expose all layer dims as config parameters so they can be overridden.

risk_assessment.description: For large K (e.g. K=32, H=5), multiplying 5 probabilities can underflow to zero. Forward pass must use log-space arithmetic.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement Step 5 of CatNat forward in log-space (sum of logs rather than product of probs). CatNat.log_prob() is the primary internal method; CatNat.forward() calls exp(log_prob()). Add numerical tests checking probs sum to 1 within 1e-6 for K in {8,16,32,64}.

risk_assessment.description: GSL model uses a GCN but layer count, hidden dim, and output dim are not fully specified. Paper says 'identical architecture' to the data-generating model but the data-generating model specs are also underspecified.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use standard 2-layer GCN with hidden dim 64 as a reasonable default. Expose all architecture parameters in config. Reference Kipf & Welling (2017) GCN as the baseline implementation.

risk_assessment.description: RL results are highly sensitive to hyperparameters. The paper uses a 160-trial TPE search and reports only the top-10 configs. Without this search, reproduced results may differ significantly.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement full TPE search via Optuna in scripts/run_rl_tpe.py. Also provide a 'fast reproduce' mode with the reported best hyperparameter ranges for Breakout and Seaquest as defaults, so users can skip the search if compute is limited.

risk_assessment.description: Paper refers to 'Breakout and Seaquest' environments without specifying v4 vs v5 or NoFrameskip variants. ALE versions affect results.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4 and SeaquestNoFrameskip-v4 (standard for PPO benchmarking per Huang et al. 2022 codebase that the paper references). Document this choice in README.

risk_assessment.description: The Energy Score (EQ10) requires M*(M-1) pairwise distance computations, which is O(M^2) in memory. M=32 produces 32*31=992 pairs per batch — manageable but worth noting.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement EnergyScore with broadcasting to avoid explicit loops. For M=32, the pairwise matrix is [32,31,B,D] which fits comfortably in GPU memory for typical batch sizes.
